headline: Your investor waterfall journey.
subheadline: A scrollable GTMx strategy artifact for routing Washington real estate investors into the right rebate-led offer.
productSummary: WithJoy.AI is an AI-powered real estate brokerage in Washington. Joy runs the home search and drafts offers, while HALO, a licensed human review layer, signs off on material decisions.
coreInsight: Because the rebate happens per transaction, its value compounds for anyone who buys repeatedly. Washington real estate investors are the clearest cold-outbound ICP.

sections.label: Signal
sections.title: The signal that proves investor intent
sections.takeaway: County deed records prove who invests, how often they buy, and which tier they should enter. The TAM and routing signal come from the same source.

sections.label: Waterfall
sections.title: One cascade routes every WA investor
sections.takeaway: Portfolio size plus acquisition recency creates exclusive tiers, from repeat buyers to scaled dormant portfolios to emerging investors and catch-all entities.

sections.label: Tiers
sections.title: Each investor segment gets a distinct offer
sections.takeaway: The hotter the transaction signal, the more concrete the rebate math. Softer segments get calculators and deal-alert entry points.

sections.label: Campaigns
sections.title: Twenty experiments layered on the waterfall
sections.takeaway: Core campaigns carry the volume. Triggered and niche plays add urgency only when the signal is specific enough.

sections.label: Email Copy
sections.title: Copy built around a concrete number
sections.takeaway: Every sequence makes the rebate visible, then asks for a tiny reply: LEDGER, YES, or CALC.

sections.label: Launch
sections.title: Launch the clearest money proof first
sections.takeaway: Start with repeat buyers and the personalized Rebate Ledger, then phase in deal alerts, emerging investors, and the catch-all calculator.

signalReasons: County deed records reveal owner of record, property count, deed dates, owner occupancy, and cash-versus-financed purchases.
The same source identifies the TAM and the routing signal, so there is no expensive qualification enrichment step.
Portfolio size and recent buying activity map directly to rebate value and urgency.
Every investor still gets a route, even when recency and portfolio depth are weak.

sources.name: County Assessor / Recorder deed data
sources.delivers: Owner of record, property count, deed dates, occupancy, cash vs. financed

sources.name: PropStream / BatchLeads
sources.delivers: Queryable investor database, portfolio counts, absentee and cash-buyer flags, estimated equity

sources.name: Skip-trace / contact enrichment
sources.delivers: Email and mobile resolution after the deed record qualifies the lead

sources.name: MLS / Zillow / Redfin
sources.delivers: Live deal matches for Joy Deal Alert campaigns

sources.name: Public trigger datasets
sources.delivers: 1031 timing, trustee sales, STR permits, REIA directories, and license rosters

routingRules.why: Remaining RE LLCs, cash buyers, absentee owners

tiers.name: Tier 1
tiers.label: In-Market Repeat Buyer
tiers.trigger: Bought 2+ WA properties in the trailing 12 months
tiers.pain: They transact often, so paying full buyer-side commission on every deal compounds into real annual leakage.
tiers.valueProp: WithJoy rebates up to 70% of the buy-side commission back at closing, roughly $15k per deal on average.

tiers.name: Tier 2
tiers.label: Scaled Portfolio, Dormant
tiers.trigger: Owns 3+ properties but bought fewer than 2 in the last 12 months
tiers.pain: Capital is waiting for the right deal, but they may re-enter and overpay on commission out of habit.
tiers.valueProp: Joy scans from a natural-language buy-box and the rebate improves the economics when they move.
tiers.replyDensity: Strong once Tier 1 proves the hook

tiers.name: Tier 3
tiers.label: Emerging Investor
tiers.pain: They have started investing and want to scale, but every acquisition dollar matters.
tiers.valueProp: The rebate makes the next purchase materially cheaper and speeds the path to door 3.

tiers.name: Catch-all
tiers.label: Remaining WA Investor Entities
tiers.trigger: Any remaining RE LLC, cash buyer, absentee owner, or investor entity
tiers.pain: They own property but lack strong recency or depth signal, so intent is softer.
tiers.valueProp: Whenever they are ready, Joy can run the search and return up to 70% of commission.

campaigns.name: Commission Rebate Calculator
campaigns.overview: Broadest net and softest CTA. Shows what a 70% rebate would return on the next purchase.

campaigns.name: Rebate Ledger
campaigns.strategy: Compute rebate they would have kept per past buy
campaigns.value: Save money, quantified from their record
campaigns.overview: The golden ICP campaign: their public deed history becomes a personalized ledger.

campaigns.name: Joy Found 3 Deals
campaigns.strategy: Infer buy-box and surface 3 live matches
campaigns.overview: Demonstrates Joy's product magic and the rebate in the same email.

campaigns.name: New LLC Welcome
campaigns.value: Save money on the first deal
campaigns.overview: A new-entity opener for investors forming budget and intent now.

campaigns.name: Repeat-Buyer Velocity
campaigns.overview: Turns recent buying velocity into concrete annual rebate math.

campaigns.name: Cash Buyer Fast-Close
campaigns.overview: Flatters sophisticated buyers who move fast and do not need hand-holding.

campaigns.name: Portfolio Profile Lookalike
campaigns.strategy: Match on portfolio size, geo, and type
campaigns.overview: Uses portfolio shape as proof before public customer logos exist.

campaigns.name: Out-of-State WA Owner
campaigns.filter: Owner mailing state differs from property state
campaigns.strategy: Reference owner vs. property geo mismatch
campaigns.overview: Joy handles remote search legwork while the investor keeps the rebate.

campaigns.name: Dormant Whale Reactivation
campaigns.strategy: Use portfolio depth and dormancy window
campaigns.overview: Reactivation angle for investors likely to re-enter with the right deal flow.

campaigns.name: 1031 Exchange Clock
campaigns.filter: Recorded a WA sale in last 30 days
campaigns.overview: A triggered burst with urgency from the reinvestment deadline.

campaigns.name: Self-Represented Buyer
campaigns.filter: Recent buy with no buyer's agent
campaigns.overview: Shows they kept independence but forfeited rebate economics.

campaigns.name: Flipper Buy-Sell Pattern
campaigns.filter: Bought and sold same parcel in under 12 months
campaigns.overview: Connects rebate dollars to spread and acquisition margin.

campaigns.name: Trustee-Sale Buyer
campaigns.overview: Bridges auction-buyer margin hunting to MLS/off-market rebate margin.

campaigns.name: Hard-Money Borrower
campaigns.filter: Private lender deed of trust or UCC
campaigns.overview: Positions rebate as relief against points and cost of capital.

campaigns.name: STR Operator
campaigns.overview: Frames rebate as furnishing budget for the next STR unit.

campaigns.name: Small-Multifamily Buyer
campaigns.overview: Uses larger price points to make the rebate feel material.

campaigns.name: Agent-Investor
campaigns.overview: Investor-first angle for licensed buyers who know what commission is worth.

campaigns.name: Relocating High-Earner
campaigns.value: Save money on first WA home
campaigns.overview: An expansion play beyond investors for high-intent first-time WA buyers.

campaigns.name: Portfolio Consolidator
campaigns.overview: Warm community-aware tone for investors actively scaling doors.

campaigns.name: The Number Audit
campaigns.overview: Shows the historical commission number and what 70% could have returned.

messages.subject: {{most_recent_address}}
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, I pulled the public record on your {{most_recent_address}} buy. You've closed {{n_purchases}} in WA in the last year. At a 70% buy-side rebate, you'd have kept about ${{est_rebate}} on that one alone. Across all {{n_purchases}}, roughly ${{cumulative_rebate}}.

That's Joy, our AI agent that runs the search and drafts the offer, with a licensed team reviewing every one before it's submitted.

Want the full ledger on your purchases? Reply LEDGER, no call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, quick math most investors like. Every ${{est_rebate}} back is capital you redeploy into the next down payment instead of handing to the buyer's side. Over a portfolio, that's a door or two.

Joy handles the grind, so your time goes to underwriting, not tour scheduling.

Should I send the ledger, or is someone else at {{company_name}} running acquisitions?

messages.subject: question for {{first_name}}
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, on your next WA property, do you already have a way to claw back the buyer's-agent commission?

Most investors don't, and it's the one closing cost that's fully negotiable. We rebate up to 70%. Averages about $15k a deal.

No call needed. Reply YES and I'll send your rebate ledger. Should I send it to you, or whoever handles acquisitions?

messages.subject: built this for you
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, last note. I built the ledger anyway. Every WA purchase under {{company_name}}, the commission paid, and what a 70% rebate returns going forward.

Joy did the math, no human wrote it.

Want me to send it over?

messages.subject: 3 in {{metro}}
threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, when you re-enter, the buyer-side commission is the easiest cost to cut.

We rebate up to 70% of it. Roughly $15k back per deal, straight into your next down payment instead of the listing side.

Joy keeps scanning {{metro}} in the background either way. Want it watching your buy-box?

messages.subject: off-market in {{metro}}
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, most deals worth buying in {{metro}} never sit on the MLS long.

Joy watches both on- and off-market, matches them to your buy-box, and drafts the offer with a licensed team reviewing it. You also keep up to 70% of the buyer-side commission.

Want me to turn on a free deal alert for your box? Reply YES.

messages.subject: free alert for {{metro}}
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, last one. I'll set up a free Joy deal alert for {{metro}} on your buy-box, no commitment.

You'll see what's out there and what a 70% rebate returns on each.

Want it on?

messages.subject: your buyer's commission
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, you own {{properties_owned_count}} in {{county}}. Most WA investors don't realize the buyer's-agent commission is negotiable and can come back to them.

With Joy you keep up to 70% of it. About $15k on the average deal. AI runs the search, a licensed team reviews every offer before it's submitted.

Want a 20-second calculator showing what you'd get back on your next purchase? Reply CALC.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, simplest way to see it.

Next WA purchase, roughly $15k of the commission comes back to you instead of staying on the listing side.

Same house, same price, you just keep more. Joy does the legwork. Want the calculator?

messages.subject: question for {{first_name}}
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, when WA investors buy their next property through us, they keep up to 70% of the buyer-side commission. Averages about $15k.

Joy, our AI agent, handles the search and the offer. A licensed team signs off before anything goes out.

Want the calculator for your numbers? Reply YES, no call needed.

messages.subject: two-minute calculator
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, last note from me. If now's not the time, here's a free calculator that shows what a 70% commission rebate returns on a WA purchase at your price point.

Two minutes, no call.

Want me to send it?

launchPlan: Build the WA investor TAM from county deeds / PropStream, run the routing spec, and skip-trace contacts.
Warm domains and inboxes for roughly two weeks before first sends.
Launch Tier 1 first with the Rebate Ledger campaign and variants.
Phase in Tier 2 once Tier 1 shows at least 1 positive reply per 300 contacts.
Add Tier 3 and the catch-all, broadening copy and softening the CTA as intent weakens.
Layer agent recruiting and 1031 trigger campaigns only after the core waterfall is stable.

thresholds.action: Good. Let it run and phase the next tier.
Borderline. Rotate the lead magnet or first-line angle.
Cut the tier and rework trigger or offer.
